title: Tahchin (Saffron Rice Cake)
description: A stunning, savory Persian rice cake baked to golden perfection with a crispy crust (tahdig) and layered with yogurt, saffron, and tender chicken.

instructions.text: Parboil the basmati rice in salted water until the grains are slightly soft on the outside but firm inside. Drain and rinse.
In a bowl, whisk together the plain yogurt, egg yolks, bloomed saffron, melted butter, and half the salt.
Mix half of the parboiled rice into the yogurt-saffron mixture to create the tahdig layer base.
In a small pan, briefly sauté the barberries in a little oil with the sugar to plump them up. Mix lightly with the cooked chicken breast.
Brush the bottom and sides of a baking dish with oil. Press the yogurt-rice mixture firmly into the bottom.
Add a layer of the chicken and barberry mixture, avoiding the edges. Top with the remaining plain parboiled rice and press down firmly.
Cover tightly with foil, poke a few holes for steam, and bake at 375°F (190°C) until the bottom and sides are deeply golden and crispy.

tips: Pressing the rice layers down firmly is the secret to a tahchin that holds its shape when inverted.
storage_notes: Store leftovers in an airtight container for up to 4 days. Reheat in the oven to re-crisp the tahdig.

substitutions.note: Use fried or roasted eggplant slices for a vegetarian tahchin.
Greek yogurt works well due to its thick consistency.
